target.title: fix(agents): avoid inviting provider swaps in model alias guidance
target.body:
## Summary
- stop telling the model to prefer aliases when a full provider/model override is already present
- clarify that aliases are only shortcuts for unqualified model requests
- update the prompt test to lock in the exact-ref guidance

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: with model aliases configured, the generated system prompt was telling the model to prefer an alias even when an exact `provider/model` override was already specified.

Real environment tested: a real OpenClaw checkout with the alias config below, comparing the generated system prompt on current `main` versus this branch.

Config used:
```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    models:
      github-copilot/gpt-4.1:
        alias: copilot-4.1
      openai/gpt-4.1:
        alias: openai-4.1
```

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. generated the system prompt with the config above on current `main`
2. generated it again on this branch
3. compared the model-alias guidance line and alias entries

Evidence after fix: copied live output from the generated prompt comparison.
Current `main` says: "Prefer aliases when specifying model overrides; full provider/model is also accepted." It then lists `copilot-4.1: github-copilot/gpt-4.1` and `openai-4.1: openai/gpt-4.1`.
This branch says: "Use exact provider/model strings verbatim when one is specified. Aliases are shortcuts for unqualified model requests." It then lists the same alias mappings.

Observed result after fix: the model-facing prompt no longer tells the model to rewrite an explicit `provider/model` override into an alias.

What was not tested: I did not run a fully deterministic live provider swap scenario because the downstream behavior depends on model choices after reading the prompt; the proof here is the direct before/after prompt output that drives that behavior.

## Testing
- `pnpm vitest src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

Closes #61949

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-338987468",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```



Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --reporter=dot
2 Vitest shards passed: 215 tests passed
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: Add channel turn delivery and control-lane diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a small, source-level Agentic Kernel spike for OpenClaw channel turns. The goal is not a rewrite: it makes Telegram/direct-message delivery and control-lane health observable and testable through existing runtime paths.

Key pieces:
- Adds turn event state modeling for received messages, turn lifecycle, delivery requirements, delivery sent/failed, and tool call/result causality.
- Wires optional turn event recording into the existing channel turn kernel.
- Adds control-lane priority helpers and tests for direct Telegram-like turns.
- Adds gateway stability and channel-turn latency diagnostics for health/status/doctor surfaces.
- Tightens stale task-flow audit handling for ended flows.
- Keeps ordinary private Telegram DMs on the keyed lane while allowing only direct control-style DMs and authorized abort/stop messages to bypass the inbound debounce key.
- Keeps the spike internals out of the public plugin SDK contract.

## Verification

Prepared and tested locally on the source branch:
- targeted Vitest runtime suite: green
- `pnpm tsgo:test`: green
- `pnpm lint:extensions:no-plugin-sdk-internal`: green
- `pnpm lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported`: green
- `git diff --check`: green
- Strong secret-pattern scans over the PR file set and final local diff found no `ops_`, OpenAI-style API keys, GitHub tokens, private keys, or AWS access keys.

## Maintainer-facing diagnostics context

This PR intentionally adds two operator-visible runtime diagnostics fields:

- `runtimeControlLane`: concise control-lane health for direct-message responsiveness, active/queued work, stale TaskFlows, and channel-turn delivery signals.
- `runtimeRecommendations`: sanitized next-action guidance derived from runtime health, without raw message bodies, secrets, mail contents, or private payloads.

These fields are meant for `status`, `health`, `doctor`, and gateway-health style surfaces so an operator can distinguish "agent is slow because work is queued", "direct control lane may be degraded", and "delivery proof is missing" without reading private chat history or patching runtime state. They are additive diagnostics; they do not change plugin SDK contracts or create a new public capability registry.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
- After-fix behavior is documented from a real local OpenClaw/Mira setup, captured redacted on 2026-06-06 Europe/Berlin.
- Ordinary private Telegram/direct-message lane remains reachable and unblocked.
- Telegram delivery is constrained to the configured direct-message allowlist; group/named targets are not valid pending or successful delivery targets.
- Direct control/stop behavior has interrupt proof with ACK and stop-ACK bounds, stale output suppression, supersede/cancel state, and no side effects after cancel.
- Visible delivery is owner/closeout-gated, and specialist/subagent work does not directly deliver visible Telegram messages.

Real environment tested:
- PR: openclaw/openclaw#90248
- PR head: efb485c4b6066283678bb590d08e9badb9ab59a8
- Local source branch: codex/agentic-kernel-spike
- Installed runtime observed by status: OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (dfc42c0)
- No local dist patch: true
- No second Telegram bridge: true
- Redaction: no chat text, no Telegram message bodies, no media, no secrets, no mail contents, and no raw private payloads.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Ran fast live status against the real local OpenClaw/Mira setup after the delivery/control-lane fixes.
2. Ran delivery hygiene audit against the real setup and inspected configured direct-message delivery policy plus delivery queue target state.
3. Ran metadata-only control-lane smoke against the real setup for long-run, stop/correction, stale output blocking, supersede/cancel, and post-cancel side-effect blocking.
4. Ran full live acceptance after the delivery/control-lane fixes.
5. Inspected the official PR/Mantis state and left Mantis/Maintainer proof as the external gate.

Evidence after fix:
Copied live output, redacted runtime log excerpt, and metadata-only terminal capture from the real setup:

```json
{
  "fast_live_status": {
    "ok": true,
    "control_lane_ok": true,
    "health": {
      "telegram_connected": true,
      "telegram_running": true,
      "event_loop_degraded": false,
      "event_loop_delay_p99_ms": 21.9
    },
    "tasks": {
      "running": 0,
      "queued": 0,
      "source": "openclaw_state_sqlite"
    },
    "cron": {
      "running": 0,
      "not_ok_count": 0,
      "consecutive_error_jobs": 0
    }
  },
  "delivery_hygiene": {
    "ok": true,
    "summary": {
      "errors": 0,
      "warnings": 0
    },
    "delivery_queue_policy": {
      "config": {
        "telegram_enabled": true,
        "dm_policy_allowlist": true,
        "group_policy_disabled": true,
        "queue_interrupt": true,
        "streaming_off": true,
        "error_policy_silent": true
      },
      "queue": {
        "invalid_pending_targets": 0,
        "invalid_success_targets": 0,
        "specialist_visible_deliveries": 0,
        "recent_invalid_target_failures": 3
      }
    }
  },
  "control_lane_smoke": {
    "ok": true,
    "checks": {
      "ack_under_5s": true,
      "stop_ack_under_3s": true,
      "old_run_cancelled_or_superseded": true,
      "stale_output_blocked": true,
      "no_side_effects_after_cancel": true,
      "metadata_only": true
    }
  },
  "full_live_acceptance": {
    "ok": true,
    "mode": "live",
    "summary": {
      "pass": 74,
      "review": 0,
      "external_waiting": 1,
      "missing": 0,
      "fail": 0
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix:
- Fast live status was green: control lane ok, Telegram connected/running, event loop not degraded, tasks running 0, tasks queued 0, cron running 0, not-ok count 0.
- Delivery hygiene was green: DM allowlist enabled, group policy disabled, interrupt queue enabled, streaming off, invalid pending targets 0, invalid successful targets 0, specialist visible deliveries 0.
- Control-lane smoke was green: ACK under 5s, stop ACK under 3s, old run cancelled/superseded, stale completion blocked, no side effects after cancel, metadata only.
- Full live acceptance was green with 74 pass, 0 review, 1 external_waiting, 0 missing, 0 fail.
- The remaining external_waiting item is official maintainer/Mantis proof, not a local runtime implementation gap.

What was not tested:
- Official Telegram Desktop visible proof for ordinary private text DM ordering was not completed by this contributor account.
- Official captioned-media private DM visible proof was not completed by this contributor account.
- Official busy-lane stop/direct-control bypass visible proof was not completed by this contributor account.
- These require maintainer/write/admin Mantis trigger or signoff. Local evidence is not claimed as a replacement for maintainer/Mantis proof.



## Official live-proof gate

The remaining external proof requested by ClawSweeper is specifically the official Mantis Telegram Desktop visible proof:

- ordinary private Telegram text DM remains on the keyed inbound lane and gets visible delivery;
- captioned-media private Telegram DM remains ordered and gets visible delivery without leaking message/media contents;
- direct control/stop-style private Telegram DM bypasses a busy lane and gets visible delivery.

I attempted the official paths from the contributor account:

- `Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof` via workflow dispatch was rejected by GitHub with `HTTP 403: Must have admin rights to Repository`.
- The accepted PR comment trigger `@openclaw-mantis telegram desktop proof` started workflow run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27016192050, but the proof jobs were skipped because GitHub reported the PR actor as `read`:

```text
Actor sbuchberger permission: read
Workflow requires write/maintain/admin access. Actor "sbuchberger" has "read".
```

Therefore this PR does not claim that local canary output is a substitute for the official Telegram Desktop proof. A maintainer/write actor needs to re-run the Mantis command, re-apply the `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` label, or trigger `Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof` via workflow_dispatch for PR `90248` / head `efb485c4b6066283678bb590d08e9badb9ab59a8`.

## Notes

This is intentionally update-safe/source-first work. It does not patch the installed OpenClaw `dist` runtime and does not add a second runtime or Telegram bridge.

target.title: [Bug]: Gateway restart during active Codex channel turn drops final reply after model.completed
target.body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

When the gateway restarts while an active channel-backed Codex turn is in flight, the turn can reach `model.completed` but then end with `codex app-server client closed before turn completed`, leaving the user with no final channel reply.

Important contrast: this does not appear to be a general "gateway restarts always break active chat" problem. In operator experience, active turns in the default OpenClaw UI/web chat often recover or pick up where they left off after a gateway restart. The observed gap is that Telegram/channel-backed turns do not get the same final-reply recovery.

### Steps to reproduce

Observed live on a VPS rather than from a minimal clean-room repro:

1. Run OpenClaw `2026.6.1` as a systemd user gateway service with Telegram channel sessions and the OpenAI Codex runtime.
2. In a Telegram topic, ask the agent to enable/use the bundled browser plugin.
3. The agent updates config / enables the plugin, which requires or triggers a gateway restart.
4. While the same Telegram turn is still active, the gateway receives `SIGTERM` and shuts down.
5. Observe that the session trajectory records `model.completed`, followed immediately by `session.ended status=error` with `promptError="codex app-server client closed before turn completed"`.
6. No final Telegram reply is delivered, even though the requested config/plugin change appears to have succeeded.

### Expected behavior

If a config change requires a gateway restart during a channel-backed turn, OpenClaw should either:

- defer the restart until the active run has delivered its final channel reply, or
- persist/resume the final reply after restart, matching the recovery behavior users see in the default OpenClaw UI/web chat, or
- send a clear channel-visible failure/retry message instead of silently dropping the completion.

### Actual behavior

The active Telegram turn was cut off during gateway shutdown. The user saw the bot stop replying even though the requested work had mostly completed.

A later retry after the gateway was stable worked, which strongly suggests this is not a Telegram ingress/routing failure and not a browser plugin enablement failure.

The default OpenClaw UI/web chat has previously appeared to tolerate gateway restarts better by continuing/recovering the active work after the service returns. The Telegram topic did not recover the final answer, so this looks like a channel-backed session delivery/resume gap rather than gateway restart itself being fundamentally impossible to handle.

### OpenClaw version

`2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)`

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS

### Install method

`npm` global install, systemd user service (`openclaw-gateway.service`)

### Model

`openai/gpt-5.5` via OpenAI Codex runtime

### Provider / routing chain

OpenClaw Gateway -> Codex app-server -> OpenAI

### Additional provider/model setup details

Telegram channel session using direct execution and OpenAI Codex runtime. A fallback model was configured, but the observed failure happened because the Codex app-server client was closed by gateway shutdown/restart, not because the provider failed to respond.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

Redacted live VPS evidence from one affected Telegram topic:

```text
2026-06-05T21:36:28Z [reload] config change detected; evaluating reload (tools.alsoAllow, plugins.allow, plugins.entries.browser, browser)
2026-06-05T21:36:29Z [reload] config change requires gateway restart (browser) — deferring until 2 operation(s), 1 reply(ies), 1 embedded run(s) complete
2026-06-05T21:36:55Z [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-06-05T21:36:55Z [agent/embedded] codex app-server client closed before turn completed
2026-06-05T21:36:55Z [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=session:<redacted-telegram-topic> durationMs=189449 error="Error: codex app-server client closed before turn completed"
```

A later retry in the same topic proved the topic/channel route itself still worked:

```text
2026-06-05T21:49:19Z [telegram] Inbound message <same-redacted-topic> ("hello?")
2026-06-05T21:49:38Z trajectory: session.ended status=success
2026-06-05T21:49:40Z [telegram] sendMessage ok
```

Then the same no-final-reply pattern reproduced again when a gateway restart happened during the active turn:

```text
2026-06-05T21:49:57Z [telegram] Inbound message <same-redacted-topic>
2026-06-05T21:50:40Z [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-06-05T21:50:40Z [agent/embedded] codex app-server client closed before turn completed
2026-06-05T21:50:40Z [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=session:<redacted-telegram-topic> durationMs=42953 error="Error: codex app-server client closed before turn completed"
2026-06-05T21:50:40Z trajectory: model.completed
2026-06-05T21:50:40Z trajectory: session.ended status=error promptError="codex app-server client closed before turn completed"
```

One more retry also hit the same shutdown window:

```text
2026-06-05T22:03:13Z [telegram] Inbound message <same-redacted-topic>
2026-06-05T22:03:18Z [telegram] sendMessage ok  # interim/progress message only
2026-06-05T22:03:40Z [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-06-05T22:03:40Z [agent/embedded] codex app-server client closed before turn completed
2026-06-05T22:03:41Z [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=session:<redacted-telegram-topic> durationMs=26873 error="Error: codex app-server client closed before turn completed"
2026-06-05T22:03:40Z trajectory: model.completed
2026-06-05T22:03:40Z trajectory: session.ended status=error promptError="codex app-server client closed before turn completed"
```

Browser/plugin state after the restart looked good, supporting that the work itself succeeded but the final channel response was lost:

```json
{
  "plugin-enabled": "pass",
  "profile": "openclaw via cdp",
  "managed-executable": "chromium at /usr/bin/chromium-browser",
  "status": {
    "enabled": true,
    "driver": "openclaw",
    "transport": "cdp",
    "headless": true
  }
}
```

Operator observation for comparison: the default OpenClaw UI/web chat appears able to continue or recover after gateway restart in similar restart-after-config-change situations. I do not yet have matching UI trajectory logs for that contrast, but it is important context for the likely boundary.

### Impact and severity

Affected: observed on Telegram topic sessions, but the failing boundary is gateway restart / Codex app-server lifecycle, so this may affect other channel-backed sessions too.

Severity: medium/high. The requested work may complete, but the user receives no final answer and has to guess whether the operation succeeded.

Frequency: intermittent, tied to restarts/config reloads while an active run is in progress. Observed multiple times in one live session.

Consequence: missed replies, duplicated user retries, confusing fallback/error diagnostics, and poor reliability for agents that modify config or enable plugins from chat.

### Additional information

This is near other Codex app-server lifecycle reports such as #86214 and #89742, but the observed shape here is narrower: the gateway restart/config reload path closes the app-server client while a channel turn is still active, and the final channel reply is not recovered even when the model has completed.

The key distinction from a generic restart problem is that the default OpenClaw UI/web chat seems to have a more durable recovery/resume path across gateway restarts. The likely fix may therefore live in channel-backed turn finalization, queued reply persistence, or replay/resume after restart, rather than in preventing all gateway restarts.

Short-term workaround: retry after the gateway is stable, and avoid restart-requiring config/plugin changes while waiting for an important channel reply.

I plan to pick this up and open a PR for the fix.

target.title: Fix realtime voice-call barge-in cancellation
target.body:
## Summary

- Move realtime voice-call barge-in cancellation from local MuLaw speech detection to the provider VAD `input_audio_buffer.speech_started` event.
- Guard cancellation with `talk.outputAudioActive` so user speech only cancels an active assistant audio response.
- Keep local MuLaw speech detection as diagnostic logging while still forwarding input audio deltas.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts -t "barge-in cancellation"`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Realtime voice-call barge-in should cancel active assistant audio from provider VAD `input_audio_buffer.speech_started`, while local MuLaw speech detection should not cancel turns by itself.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw voice-call setup on macOS, built from branch `fix/realtime-provider-barge-in` at commit `6896d3b`, gateway restarted via LaunchAgent, Twilio voice-call plugin with OpenAI realtime provider.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm build`, ran `openclaw gateway restart`, then started a live conversation call with `openclaw voicecall call --mode conversation --message "Hallo Martin, dies ist der Real Behavior Proof fuer den Realtime Barge-in Fix. Bitte unterbrich mich jetzt waehrend ich spreche, damit ich pruefen kann, ob das Provider Speech Started Event die Ausgabe sauber abbricht."`. During assistant playback, the human tester interrupted the call.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (6896d3b)
  callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID]

  2026-06-05T23:20:10.793+02:00 talk event output.audio.started
  2026-06-05T23:20:12.590+02:00 [voice-call] realtime local speech detected callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID] providerCallId=[REDACTED_PROVIDER_CALL_ID]
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.008+02:00 [voice-call] realtime outbound audio cleared by provider barge-in callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID] providerCallId=[REDACTED_PROVIDER_CALL_ID] queuedBytes=63840
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.008+02:00 talk event output.audio.done
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.009+02:00 talk event turn.cancelled
  ```

- Observed result after fix: Interrupting while assistant audio was active caused provider barge-in handling to clear queued outbound audio and emit `turn.cancelled`; the local MuLaw detector logged speech but did not perform the cancellation itself.
- What was not tested: Other realtime providers, non-Twilio providers, and browser/UI talk transports were not live-tested in this proof.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Evidence is from one local macOS OpenClaw setup and one live phone call; sensitive call identifiers are redacted. Unit coverage in this PR exercises the provider VAD event path separately.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, the implementation cancelled from local MuLaw speech detection and did not cancel from provider `input_audio_buffer.speech_started`; this was verified by code inspection and covered by updating the existing barge-in unit test.

target.title: fix(agents): hydrate allowlisted dynamic model metadata
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes gateway/model catalog metadata for exact allowlisted dynamic models such as `ollama/minimax-m3:cloud`.
- Full catalog loads now hydrate exact `agents.defaults.models` refs even when discovery already returned a sparse provider/id row, so live `/api/show` metadata carries `reasoning: true` into catalog and thinking validation.
- Gateway/session patch validation and agent-command early thinking validation now request or hydrate full catalog metadata before rejecting explicit thinking levels.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90315 reports that local Ollama dynamic resolution accepts `--thinking low`, while the gateway path rejects the same model as off-only unless users duplicate metadata under `models.providers.ollama.models[]`.

What is the intended outcome?

- An exact allowlisted dynamic Ollama model can keep provider-discovered thinking metadata in the runtime catalog without adding manual provider model rows.
- Existing discovered catalog entries remain available even if one dynamic allowlist metadata lookup fails.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No new config, env var, migration, or Ollama-specific hardcoded model id.
- No change to read-only catalog browse behavior; dynamic provider hooks still run only on full catalog loads.

What does success look like?

- Gateway thinking validation can see the same `reasoning: true` metadata that dynamic provider resolution discovers for an exact allowlisted model.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether full catalog load is the right owner boundary for materializing exact allowlisted dynamic model metadata.
- Whether per-ref failure isolation and non-cacheable partial gateway catalog handling are sufficient for transient provider/API failures.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90315

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #90138, but that PR fixes MiniMax provider thinking behavior and does not touch the Ollama gateway catalog path.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; issue is labeled queueable/fix-shape-clear.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Exact allowlisted dynamic Ollama models can lose live-discovered `reasoning` metadata in the gateway catalog and agent-command thinking validation paths, making `--thinking low` fail as unsupported even though dynamic provider resolution can run the model.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway from this branch on macOS, with an isolated temporary `HOME` / `OPENCLAW_HOME` / `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and a temporary local Ollama-compatible HTTP server. The server returned `minimax-m3:cloud` from `/api/tags`, returned `/api/show` metadata with `model_info["llama.context_length"] = 1048576` and `capabilities: ["thinking", "tools", "completion"]`, and returned a native Ollama `/api/chat` streaming response.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Start a local Ollama-compatible server on `127.0.0.1:<temp-port>`.
  2. Write temporary config with only `agents.defaults.models["ollama/minimax-m3:cloud"] = {}` and provider `models.providers.ollama.baseUrl = http://127.0.0.1:<temp-port>`.
  3. Start gateway with `node dist/index.js gateway run --allow-unconfigured --bind loopback --port <temp-gateway-port> --auth token --token <local-proof-token> --compact`.
  4. Run:
     ```sh
     OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:<temp-gateway-port> \
     OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<local-proof-token> \
     node dist/index.js infer model run --gateway \
       --model ollama/minimax-m3:cloud \
       --thinking low \
       --prompt "Reply with exactly: pong" \
       --json
     ```
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  Terminal capture copied live output from the gateway proof:

  ```json
  {
    "status": 0,
    "stdout": {
      "ok": true,
      "capability": "model.run",
      "transport": "gateway",
      "provider": "ollama",
      "model": "minimax-m3:cloud",
      "attempts": [],
      "outputs": [
        {
          "text": "pong",
          "mediaUrl": null
        }
      ]
    },
    "requestUrls": [
      "/api/tags",
      "/api/show",
      "/api/show body:{\"name\":\"minimax-m3:cloud\"}",
      "/api/tags",
      "/api/show",
      "/api/show body:{\"name\":\"minimax-m3:cloud\"}",
      "/api/chat"
    ],
    "chatBody": {
      "model": "minimax-m3:cloud",
      "stream": true,
      "options": {},
      "think": "low"
    }
  }
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The gateway command accepted `--thinking low`, hydrated `/api/show` metadata for the exact allowlisted `minimax-m3:cloud` model, called `/api/chat` with `think: "low"`, and returned `ok: true` with `pong`.
- What was not tested: Live Ollama Cloud credentials/service for real `minimax-m3:cloud` were not used; the gateway proof used a local Ollama-compatible server.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof server is local and intentionally synthetic, but it exercises the real OpenClaw gateway, catalog hydration, agent-command thinking validation, Ollama provider hook, and Ollama chat request path.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The issue includes the before failure: gateway rejects `--thinking low` with `Thinking level "low" is not supported for ollama/minimax-m3:cloud. Use one of: off.` when only `agents.defaults.models` contains the model.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.thinking-e2e.test.ts src/gateway/server-model-catalog.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/agent-command.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.thinking-e2e.test.ts src/gateway/server-model-catalog.ts src/gateway/server-model-catalog.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-command.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.thinking-e2e.test.ts src/gateway/server-model-catalog.ts src/gateway/server-model-catalog.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm openclaw --help` (rebuilt local dist before gateway proof)
- Local gateway proof command above
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts` covers exact allowlisted dynamic metadata hydration when the model is missing and when discovery already returned a sparse row.
- `src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts` covers early explicit thinking validation hydrating full catalog metadata before rejection.
- `src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts` covers session thinking validation requesting the full gateway catalog.
- `src/gateway/server-model-catalog.test.ts` covers not caching partial full catalog loads.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Gateway model runs could reject `ollama/minimax-m3:cloud --thinking low` as off-only because early validation saw only sparse configured/allowlist catalog rows.

If no test was added, why not?

- Regression tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No new auth or secret surfaces. Full catalog loads may call existing provider dynamic model hooks for exact configured model refs; read-only browse paths remain side-effect-free.

What is the highest-risk area?

Full model catalog loads and early agent-command thinking validation now perform optional dynamic metadata hydration for exact allowlisted refs.

How is that risk mitigated?

The behavior is limited to non-read-only/full metadata paths, uses existing provider dynamic hooks, avoids caching partial gateway full-catalog failures, and isolates per-ref hydration failures so base catalog entries and other dynamic refs remain available.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

CI and ClawSweeper re-review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and ClawSweeper re-review. A maintainer or reporter with Ollama Cloud credentials can add true cloud-service proof for `minimax-m3:cloud`.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Local autoreview was clean after the latest patch.
- ClawSweeper proof feedback was addressed with a real local gateway proof that reaches `/api/show`, sends `/api/chat` with `think: "low"`, and returns `pong`.

target.title: fix: carry reply metadata into runtime context
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a first-class current-turn reply metadata payload to `CurrentInboundPromptContext` so Telegram/forum reply facts survive the reply prompt/runtime handoff as structured OpenClaw metadata.

This is the Morty patch shape, polished for upstream:

- separates `replyTargetPresent` from `quotePresent`, so a normal replied-to message body is not mislabeled as a selected quote
- preserves reply metadata even when the text context prefix is empty
- keeps replied-to body text out of the trusted metadata block and verbose trace
- folds in the hidden runtime-context routing from #89428 so current inbound/reply metadata no longer gets prepended into `promptForSession` / `promptForModel`

## Related

Addresses the runtime handoff portion of #88032.

Builds on the same routing direction as #89428. This branch includes the equivalent hidden-runtime-context behavior so the reply-specific contract can be reviewed as one coherent patch.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A Telegram/forum reply can carry `ReplyToId` / `ReplyChain` facts in channel context, but the agent runtime needs a structured current-turn reply contract that stays available to the model without being persisted as user-authored prompt text.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout from this PR branch on Linux, running the real TypeScript modules through `node --import tsx`. The proof uses redacted Telegram/forum-style ids.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** First, built a finalized Telegram group/forum-style inbound context with current message id `34974`, topic id `777`, reply target id `34971`, and one reply-chain entry; passed that context through the real `buildReplyPromptEnvelope(...)` prompt-prelude path; rendered the current-turn runtime prefix with `buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(...)`; checked that structured reply metadata appears before untrusted context and that the trusted reply metadata block does not include the replied-to body text.

```bash
PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node --import tsx -e 'const { finalizeInboundContext } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-context.ts"); const { buildReplyPromptEnvelope } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.ts"); const { buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix } = await import("./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts"); const sessionCtx = finalizeInboundContext({ Body: "Sean, answer this old reply", BodyStripped: "Sean, answer this old reply", Provider: "telegram", Surface: "telegram", OriginatingChannel: "telegram", ChatType: "group", MessageSid: "34974", MessageThreadId: 777, ReplyToId: "34971", ReplyToBody: "old target body", ReplyToSender: "Alice", ReplyChain: [{ messageId: "34971", threadId: "777", sender: "Alice", body: "old target body" }] }); const envelope = buildReplyPromptEnvelope({ ctx: sessionCtx, sessionCtx, baseBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", prefixedBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", hasUserBody: true, inboundUserContext: "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971", isBareSessionReset: false, startupAction: "new" }); const prefix = buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(envelope.currentInboundContext); console.log(JSON.stringify({ reply: envelope.currentInboundContext?.reply, text: envelope.currentInboundContext?.text, metadataBeforeUntrusted: prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") >= 0 && prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") < prefix.indexOf("Conversation info"), metadataLeaksReplyBody: prefix.slice(0, prefix.indexOf("Conversation info")).includes("old target body") }, null, 2));'
```

Then, exercised the hidden runtime-context routing with the real `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts(...)` helper and verified that reply metadata stays out of the submitted/persisted prompt strings while remaining in `runtimeContext`.

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'PROOF'
import { resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts';
const parts = resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts({
  effectivePrompt: 'what is this referring to?',
  transcriptPrompt: 'what is this referring to?',
  currentInboundContext: {
    text: 'Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=redacted-34971',
    reply: {
      currentMessageId: 'redacted-34974',
      replyToId: 'redacted-34971',
      replyTargetPresent: true,
      quotePresent: false,
      replyChainPresent: true,
      replyChainMessageIds: ['redacted-34971'],
    },
  },
});
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  prompt: parts.prompt,
  modelPrompt: parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt,
  promptContainsReplyMetadata: parts.prompt.includes('Current reply metadata') || parts.prompt.includes('redacted-34971'),
  modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata: (parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt).includes('Current reply metadata') || (parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt).includes('redacted-34971'),
  runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata: Boolean(parts.runtimeContext?.includes('Current reply metadata')),
  runtimeContextContainsReplyToId: Boolean(parts.runtimeContext?.includes('redacted-34971')),
}, null, 2));
PROOF
```

**Evidence after fix:** Console output from the first real module command:

```json
{
  "reply": {
    "currentMessageId": "34974",
    "threadId": "777",
    "replyToId": "34971",
    "replyTargetPresent": true,
    "quotePresent": false,
    "replyChainPresent": true,
    "replyChainMessageIds": ["34971"]
  },
  "text": "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971",
  "metadataBeforeUntrusted": true,
  "metadataLeaksReplyBody": false
}
```

Console output from the hidden runtime-context proof:

```json
{
  "prompt": "what is this referring to?",
  "modelPrompt": "what is this referring to?",
  "promptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyToId": true
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** Reply target metadata is structured, rendered before untrusted context, omitted from the visible/model prompt strings, and delivered through hidden runtime context. The trusted reply metadata block does not leak the replied-to body text.


**Live Telegram/forum transport proof (redacted):** A real Telegram forum reply was sent in an existing group topic on 2026-06-05 UTC. No live OpenClaw install or runtime was changed for this transport proof.

Redacted inbound metadata supplied to the agent turn:

```json
{
  "channel": "telegram",
  "surface": "telegram",
  "chat_type": "group",
  "is_forum": true,
  "topic_id": "<redacted-topic>",
  "message_id": "<redacted-current>",
  "reply_to_id": "<redacted-anchor>",
  "was_mentioned": true,
  "has_reply_context": true
}
```

The assembled current message included a Telegram reply target for the proof anchor while the persisted session user message remained the user-authored text only:

```json
{
  "role": "user",
  "sourceChannel": "telegram",
  "content": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745"
}
```

Then the PR branch was exercised with the same redacted Telegram/forum shape through the real `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts(...)` helper:

```json
{
  "prompt": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745",
  "modelPrompt": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745",
  "promptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyToId": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsForumSignal": true
}
```

This pairs a real Telegram/forum reply event with the PR-branch runtime handoff: the transport supplies `reply_to_id`/forum/mention facts, the session prompt remains clean user text, and the PR branch keeps reply/forum metadata in hidden runtime context instead of visible/model prompt text.

**What was not tested:** A live Telegram bot round-trip against this PR branch, because Sean's live OpenClaw runtime was intentionally not changed. The local checkout does not have `rastermill` hydrated, so the broader embedded-attempt context-engine shard currently fails during media imports before reaching these assertions; I did not run dependency install as part of this PR polish.

## Tests

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts` - passed 2 Vitest shards, 36 tests
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
- attempted `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` - reply/runtime shards passed, embedded-attempt shard was blocked by missing local package `rastermill`

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix** (`ac52eb0b24`):
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).

target.title: fix(memory): resolve adapter default model in plain status identity check
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw memory status` always printed `Index identity: index was built for model <X>, expected` (blank expected) and `Vector search: paused until memory is rebuilt`, even right after a successful `openclaw memory index --force`. As a result `memory_search` tool calls returned `disabled: true, error: "index metadata is missing"`. `openclaw memory status --deep` reported the system healthy — only the plain status path was broken.

Fixes #90413.

### Root cause

`MemoryManagerSyncOps.resolveCurrentIndexIdentityState` built the *configured* provider identity from `this.settings.model` directly. For an agent with the default `memorySearch.model` (the empty string `""`), the plain `memory status` path runs **before** the provider is initialized, so `settings.model` is still that unresolved empty default. The empty string survives the existing `??`/direct assignment, so identity compared `meta.model` (e.g. `text-embedding-3-small`) against `""`, declared a mismatch, and printed a blank "expected" model. The `--deep` / index paths initialize the provider first, where the adapter default model is resolved, so they were unaffected.

### Fix

Resolve the configured model the same way provider initialization does — use the trimmed configured model when set, otherwise fall back to the adapter's default via the existing `resolveEmbeddingProviderFallbackModel` helper. This keeps the plain status identity check symmetric with the initialized path. The `refreshIndexIdentityDirty` uninitialized branch delegates to this same function, so it is fixed in one place; the initialized branch already carries the resolved `provider.model`.

### Automated tests (supplemental)

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts` — new regression test passes; all identity tests pass (5 passed).
- `oxfmt --check` clean on both changed files.

### Real behavior proof

Real `openclaw` CLI built from this source tree (`OpenClaw 2026.6.2`), isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, agent `main` with `memorySearch.provider: "openai"` and **no explicit model** (the empty-string default that triggers the bug), pointed at a local OpenAI-compatible `/v1/embeddings` endpoint so indexing runs fully offline. The exact same on-disk index is reused for both captures — only the patched binary differs, no reindex between them.

- **Behavior addressed:** plain `openclaw memory status` falsely reporting an `Index identity` mismatch with a blank "expected" model and `Vector search: paused` when `memorySearch.model` is the empty default, which in turn reports `memory_search` as disabled.
- **Real environment tested:** real `openclaw` CLI built from this source tree, run against a live local `/v1/embeddings` endpoint with an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`; the index was built once with `openclaw memory index --agent main --force` (`Memory index updated (main).`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `OPENCLAW_HOME=… openclaw memory status --agent main` (identical index on disk; the only difference between the two captures below is the patched binary).
- **Evidence after fix:** Before the patch, plain `openclaw memory status --agent main` printed the false identity mismatch and pause:

  ```
  Memory Search (main)
  Provider: openai (requested: openai)
  Indexed: 1/1 files · 1 chunks
  Dirty: yes
  Index identity: index was built for model text-embedding-3-small, expected 
  Vector search: paused until memory is rebuilt
  Fix: Run: openclaw memory status --index --agent main
  ```

  After the patch, the same on-disk index (no reindex) reports healthy — the `Index identity` and `Vector search: paused` lines are gone and `Dirty` flips to `no`:

  ```
  Memory Search (main)
  Provider: openai (requested: openai)
  Sources: memory
  Dirty: no
  By source:
  Vector store: unknown
  Vector dims: 16
  FTS: ready
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** plain `memory status` reports the existing index as valid, so `memory_search` is no longer reported as `disabled`/`index metadata is missing`. `openclaw memory search "fallback embedding provider" --agent main` returns the indexed chunk (`0.627 memory/notes.md:1-5`) in both builds, matching the report that CLI search keeps working while plain status falsely pauses.
- **What was not tested:** a Homebrew-packaged install (verified against a source build instead) and a hosted embedding endpoint (a local OpenAI-compatible `/v1/embeddings` endpoint stood in for `text-embedding-3-small`, which only affects the stored vectors, not the identity-resolution path under test).

target.title: fix(outbound): keep Discord runtime adapters resolvable
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Discord final replies can fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` when the outbound path observes a pinned/setup channel registry that no longer exposes the runtime send/message adapter while the active replacement registry does.
- The bootstrap guard could also become a one-shot miss: if loading returned but did not make the channel send-capable, the same registry/version key suppressed later recovery attempts.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90162 reports the final reply delivery failure on v2026.6.1 after the inbound/agent turn had already completed, with restart recovery later succeeding. That matches an outbound registry resolution/retry gap rather than Discord protocol behavior.

What is the intended outcome?

- Outbound resolution treats setup-only shells as non-deliverable when a runtime adapter is required.
- Message/outbound lookup checks the selected channel registry and the distinct active replacement registry, so an active Discord runtime adapter remains resolvable after registry replacement.
- Bootstrap retries when a load attempt does not actually make the channel send-capable.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Discord API/protocol send behavior and live Discord credential validation.
- Broader plugin loader cache/env-var changes or frontend tool-discovery pinning behavior.

What does success look like?

- Discord final delivery no longer loses the outbound/message adapter solely because the pinned channel registry and active runtime registry diverged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the runtime-vs-setup-shell distinction is narrow enough for outbound delivery paths.
- Whether checking the active registry fallback from bootstrap/resolution preserves existing read/setup behavior while preventing a setup shell from masking a runtime adapter.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90162

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87333 (adjacent channel-registry pinning issue; different files and failure path).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request seen; ClawSweeper marked the issue source-repro/fix-shape-clear.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  Discord final reply delivery could fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` after active registry replacement when the selected channel registry still exposed only a setup shell and the bootstrap attempt guard did not retry after a non-send-capable load.
- Real environment tested:

Live Discord proof was also captured on a PR-head temp gateway running OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (`f62c3e0`) with build-info commit `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b`; the temp PR-head gateway was stopped afterward and production gateway health was verified OK.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts`

  `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts`

  `codex review --base upstream/main`

  `git -C <pr-worktree> rev-parse HEAD`

```text
No actionable regressions were identified in the changed outbound channel bootstrap/resolution paths. The added behavior is covered by targeted tests, and nearby outbound/gateway tests passed.
```

- Observed result after fix:
  Source-backed runtime tests prove that setup-only and actions-only shells do not satisfy runtime outbound resolution, active replacement registries remain visible to message/outbound resolution, and bootstrap retries after a load that still does not make the channel send-capable. Live Discord proof also shows a real Discord inbound turn completed and the final outbound Discord reply posted successfully on PR #90198 / SHA `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b` with no `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` error in the PR-head log.
- What was not tested:
  A live before/after reproduction on the exact pre-patch SHA was not run; the before behavior is covered by the linked stable-release issue logs and source-level regression tests.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  Live proof used a temporary PR-head gateway on port `18790` with a Discord tech account. Message, guild, channel, session, and run identifiers are redacted. The temp PR-head gateway was stopped after proof capture and production gateway health was verified OK.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  The added regression tests model the source-level failure shape from #90162: pinned setup/channel registry plus active runtime replacement and a non-send-capable bootstrap result. These cases would have returned/kept a setup shell or suppressed retry before the resolver/bootstrap changes.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts` — passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` — passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` — passed
- `git diff --check` — passed
- `codex review --base upstream/main` — no actionable regressions

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts` covers active replacement registry send-capability visibility and retry after a bootstrap attempt that leaves the channel non-send-capable.
- `src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts` covers pinned message adapter lookup after active registry replacement, setup shell and actions-only avoidance during bootstrap, active runtime preference over loaded setup shells, and non-return of setup shells after unsuccessful bootstrap.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- A setup-only channel shell could be returned as the outbound plugin for a runtime delivery path, masking a send-capable active runtime plugin or leaving delivery to fail as unsupported.
- Bootstrap could record an attempt even when the channel still could not send, suppressing a later retry in the same registry-version window.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Final outbound delivery should now recover through a runtime adapter instead of failing when registry replacement leaves a setup shell in the selected channel registry.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Some non-delivery callers might have expected setup-only plugin metadata from `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin({ allowBootstrap: true })`.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The stricter runtime-surface filtering applies to outbound/runtime resolution. Read-only resolver paths still preserve setup/metadata visibility, and targeted plus nearby consumer tests passed.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Ready for maintainer/ClawSweeper review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI is green on the current SHA. ClawSweeper requested live Discord proof; redacted live proof has now been added to the PR body showing inbound Discord processing and final reply delivery on `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b`.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- No PR comments yet. The implementation follows the issue hint to prioritize registry/outbound adapter resolution over Discord protocol code.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(minimax): exempt M3 from thinking-disabled wrapper
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper` injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` into every MiniMax `anthropic-messages` request. That fits M2.x (the original motivation in 2701e75f40 — mask leaky `reasoning_content` deltas), but **breaks MiniMax-M3**: against `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages`, M3 with thinking disabled returns an empty `content` array with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` and a 1-token usage.

Why does this matter now?

- 2026.6.1 ships M3 (#88860 — catalog, model definitions, provider-registration). End users on M3 currently see "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" from the embedded agent retry loop, even though the model is reachable and authenticated. The wrapper is the only thing standing between a working M3 request and an empty response.

What is the intended outcome?

- M3 (and forward-compatible M3.x) skip the wrapper. M3 emits proper Anthropic thinking blocks (`content_block_start` type `thinking` + `thinking_delta`), so the wrapper's protection is unnecessary anyway.
- M2.x behavior is unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` (still routes M2.7 → M2.7-highspeed).
- No changes to provider catalog, model definitions, or M3 routing — those landed correctly in #88860.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The model-id matcher: `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` — covers `MiniMax-M3`, `MiniMax-M3.1`, `MiniMax-M3-anything`, case-insensitive. Is this future-proof enough?

## Linked context

Related #88860 (feat(minimax): add m3 model support).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: MiniMax-M3 returns empty content from `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages` when OpenClaw injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` via `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper`. Without the fix, M3 chats surface as "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" in the embedded agent runtime.

- Real environment tested: AWS Lightsail Ubuntu 22.04 VM (8 GB / 2 vCPU, ap-south-1) running `openclaw@2026.6.1-beta.3`, MiniMax API key against the global endpoint `api.minimax.io`. Direct `curl -N` against the Anthropic-compatible MiniMax endpoint and end-to-end via the OpenClaw gateway's OpenAI-compat chat-completions route.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Direct probe against MiniMax's Anthropic-compatible endpoint, three variations:
     ```
     curl -N -sS --max-time 30 https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages        -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "x-api-key: ${MINIMAX_API_KEY}"        -H "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01"        -d '{"model":"MiniMax-M3","max_tokens":50,"thinking":{"type":"disabled"},"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":true}'
     ```
     and the same payload with `"thinking":{"type":"enabled","budget_tokens":1024}` and with no `thinking` field at all.
  2. End-to-end via OpenClaw gateway with the wrapper patched (M3 skipped):
     ```
     curl -sS --max-time 60 -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GATEWAY_TOKEN}"        -d '{"model":"openclaw/main","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":false}'        http://127.0.0.1:18789/v1/chat/completions
     ```

- After-fix evidence (live SSE captured from MiniMax + final gateway response):

  Direct MiniMax call with `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` (current broken default — empty content):
  ```
  event: message_start
  data: {"type":"message_start","message":{"id":"…","role":"assistant","content":[],"model":"MiniMax-M3","stop_reason":null,…}}
  event: ping
  event: content_block_stop
  data: {"type":"content_block_stop","index":0}
  event: message_delta
  data: {"type":"message_delta","delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"input_tokens":51,"output_tokens":1,…}}
  event: message_stop
  ```
  No `content_block_start`, no `content_block_delta` — empty response.

  Direct MiniMax call with no `thinking` field (and with `thinking:enabled`, same shape):
  ```
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":0,"content_block":{"type":"thinking","thinking":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":0,"delta":{"type":"thinking_delta","thinking":"The user simply"}}
  …
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":1,"content_block":{"type":"text","text":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"text_delta","text":"ok"}}
  event: content_block_stop
  event: message_delta
  data: {"delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"output_tokens":20,…}}
  ```
  Proper Anthropic thinking + text blocks, visible "ok" present.

  End-to-end OpenClaw gateway with this patch applied:
  ```
  {"id":"chatcmpl_8d948ff1-…","object":"chat.completion","created":…,"model":"openclaw/main","choices":[{"index":0,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"ok"},"finish_reason":"stop"}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":21985,"completion_tokens":17,"total_tokens":22002}}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: M3 returns the visible `"ok"` string in `choices[0].message.content` end-to-end. Without the fix, the same gateway path returns `"minimax/MiniMax-M3 ended with an incomplete terminal response"` (code `incomplete_result`). All eight unit tests in `src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` pass, including the two new M3 cases for `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

- What was not tested: non-Anthropic-compat MiniMax paths (none affected by this change); M2.5/M2.7 (covered by unchanged existing tests). The CN endpoint (`api.minimaxi.com`) was not exercised live but inherits the same code path and is covered by the `minimax-portal` regression test.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: API-key endpoint only on the global region; the portal OAuth path was not exercised live. The captured SSE chunks above are real responses from `api.minimax.io`, redacted only for the response id and the input-token count to remove account-correlated metadata.

## Tests and validation

- `npx vitest run src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` → 8/8 pass (including 2 new M3 cases).
- Regression coverage added: `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on anthropic-messages` and `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on minimax-portal`.
- What failed before: M3 requests returned empty `content` with `stop_reason: "end_turn"`; OpenClaw retried with the visible-answer continuation and then surfaced `incomplete_result`.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: **Yes** — M3 requests now actually return content; M2.x behavior is unchanged.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: **No**.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: **No**.
- Highest-risk area: the model-id regex matching an unexpected MiniMax id family. Mitigated by anchoring to `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` so only `MiniMax-M3*` prefixes match, with regression tests for both `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: nothing.
- Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed: clawsweeper's docs-alignment finding (added the M3 exception to `docs/providers/minimax.md` Warning + Thinking-defaults accordion, `docs/tools/thinking.md`, `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`, `docs/gateway/config-tools.md`).
